# 10 — `restart: :permanent` silently resurrects agents with fresh state

**Severity:** Medium
**Location:** `lib/jido/agent_server.ex` (`child_spec/1`), `lib/jido.ex` (`init/1` — `max_restarts: 1000, max_seconds: 5`)

## Problem

`AgentServer.child_spec/1` defaults to `restart: :permanent` under a DynamicSupervisor
configured with `max_restarts: 1000, max_seconds: 5`. When an agent crashes:

- It restarts from its **original start opts** — `resolve_agent/1` calls
  `agent.new(id: id, state: initial_state)` again. All accumulated agent state since
  startup is gone unless the user wired up the keyed lifecycle + storage (which is
  opt-in via InstanceManager). The agent keeps its registry name and ID, so peers
  holding the ID see an apparently-healthy agent that has *amnesia* — much worse for an
  autonomous system than an obviously-dead one.
- Parent linkage is partially healed (RuntimeStore rehydration in
  `hydrate_parent_from_runtime_store/1` is genuinely nice work), but in-flight directive
  queues, completion waiters, and strategy progress are unrecoverable, and nothing emits
  a "restarted, state reset" signal to the parent — the parent only gets a `ChildExit`
  for the old pid and a `ChildStarted` for the new one *if* the child's startup
  notification path runs (it does), leaving the parent to infer the reset.
- `max_restarts: 1000` in 5 seconds means a deterministic crash (bad persisted state, a
  poison signal redelivered by a cron tick) spins ~1000 times before escalating —
  hammering logs, telemetry, and any startup side effects (parent notifications,
  RuntimeStore writes, plugin `child_spec` processes).

Also: `child_spec/1` uses `id: opts[:id] || __MODULE__`. Under DynamicSupervisor the id
is ignored, but the same child_spec is the documented way to embed agents in static
supervisors — where two agents without explicit `:id`s collide on `Jido.AgentServer`.

## Why it matters

Restart-with-fresh-state is the right default for stateless workers; for stateful agents
it converts a crash into silent data loss. Users coming from GenServer experience expect
this, but the framework's pitch is "agents hold meaningful state."

## Recommendation

- Default to `restart: :transient` (restart only on abnormal exit is still true — but the
  bigger question is whether restart is desirable at all without persistence). At
  minimum, document the amnesia behavior prominently where `start_agent/3` is taught.
- Lower `max_restarts` to something sane (the OTP default is 3 in 5s) or make it an
  instance option.
- Emit a distinct `jido.agent.restarted` signal (the `restored_from_storage` flag already
  exists to distinguish thawed starts) so parents/strategies can react to state loss.
title: F10: `restart: :permanent` silently resurrects agents with fresh state
